evidence_slots.observed_behavior: Search results show acct_ctrl_i connections for reset, RSA, RNG, AES, SHA/HMAC, packet, account, reglk, and DMA wrappers, and reglk_ctrl_i slices for the same set. Bootrom request is explicitly gated by acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][0]. However, debug NoC bridge and JTAG DMI path have no visible acc_ctrl/reglk guard, CLINT generic AXI-lite path has no policy input, and PLIC request logic at lines 783-880 uses AXI valid/ready directly without observed acc_ctrl/reglk gating.
evidence_slots.path: top-level NoC ports -> per-peripheral noc_axilite_bridge/AXI paths -> wrapper or direct peripheral; guarded examples use acct_ctrl_i(acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][index]) and reglk_ctrl_i(reglk_ctrl[index*8+7:index*8]); unguarded examples include debug bridge/DMI, CLINT bridge/interface, and PLIC request logic
evidence_slots.state_condition: priv_lvl_i drives acc_ctrl_c indexing; acc_ctrl_c construction includes j==5 alias; reglk_ctrl slices are propagated to many wrappers; debug_mode_i/testmode_i are present for debug/reset but not shown as general access guards; we_flag signals affect selected wrapper lock/policy behavior
evidence_slots.impact: Peripherals with missing policy use sites can be accessed or controlled even when access-control policy would deny them, undermining central permission enforcement.

rtl_evidence.detail: acc_ctrl_c[i][j] constructed from acc_ctrl[j*4+i] with special j==5 alias.
acct_ctrl_i(acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][...]) connections found at 926,1022,1108,1195,1281,1366,1451,1536,1627,1729,1819,1906 for many wrappers.
bootrom request is gated by acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][0].
debug NoC AXI-lite bridge connects to dm_master without visible acc_ctrl/reglk gate.
PLIC request state machine drives plic_req.valid/write from plic_master valid/ready without visible acc_ctrl/reglk guard in supplied snippet.
generic CLINT AXI-lite interface has no acc_ctrl/reglk/privilege inputs and drives en_o/we_o from AXI state.

inspection_records.candidate_finding: Policy coverage is inconsistent across sensitive peripherals. Many wrapper instances receive acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][index] and reglk_ctrl slices, but debug and CLINT paths lack visible acc_ctrl/reglk guards; boot ROM has an acc_ctrl request gate only; PLIC logic shown also lacks visible acc_ctrl/reglk gating. Therefore the central policy is not consistently enforced for every protected peripheral path in the supplied evidence.
inspection_records.notes: The record is a coverage-level candidate violation; individual guarded wrappers may satisfy their local obligations, but the supplied scope shows exceptions for sensitive paths.

16: module axi_lite_interface #(
17:     parameter int unsigned AXI_ADDR_WIDTH = 64,
18:     parameter int unsigned AXI_DATA_WIDTH = 64,
19:     parameter int unsigned AXI_ID_WIDTH   = 10
20: ) (
21:     input logic                       clk_i,    // Clock
22:     input logic                       rst_ni,  // Asynchronous reset active low
23: 
24:     input  ariane_axi::req_t          axi_req_i,
25:     output ariane_axi::resp_t         axi_resp_o,
26: 
27:     output logic [AXI_ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] address_o,
28:     output logic                      en_o,        // transaction is valid
29:     output logic                      we_o,        // write
30:     input  logic [AXI_DATA_WIDTH-1:0] data_i,      // data
31:     output logic [AXI_DATA_WIDTH-1:0] data_o
32: );
33: 
34:     // The RLAST signal is not required, and is considered asserted for every transfer on the read data channel.
35:     enum logic [1:0] { IDLE, READ, WRITE, WRITE_B } state_q, state_d;
36:     // save the trans id, we will need it for reflection otherwise we are not plug compatible to the AXI standard
37:     logic [AXI_ID_WIDTH-1:0]   trans_id_n, trans_id_q;
38:     // address register
39:     logic [AXI_ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] address_n,  address_q;
40: 
41:     // pass through read data on the read data channel
42:     assign axi_resp_o.r.data = data_i;
43:     // send back the transaction id we've latched
44:     assign axi_resp_o.r.id = trans_id_q;
45:     assign axi_resp_o.b.id = trans_id_q;
46:     // set r_last to one as defined by the AXI4 - Lite standard
47:     assign axi_resp_o.r.last = 1'b1;
48:     // we do not support any errors so set response flag to all zeros
49:     assign axi_resp_o.b.resp = 2'b0;
50:     assign axi_resp_o.r.resp = 2'b0;
51:     // output data which we want to write to the slave
52:     assign data_o = axi_req_i.w.data;
53:     // ------------------------
54:     // AXI4-Lite State Machine
55:     // ------------------------
56:     always_comb begin
57:         // default signal assignment
58:         state_d    = state_q;
59:         address_n  = address_q;
60:         trans_id_n = trans_id_q;
61: 
62:         // we'll answer a write request only if we got address and data
63:         axi_resp_o.aw_ready = 1'b0;
64:         axi_resp_o.w_ready  = 1'b0;
65:         axi_resp_o.b_valid  = 1'b0;
66: 
67:         axi_resp_o.ar_ready = 1'b0;
68:         axi_resp_o.r_valid  = 1'b0;
69: 
70:         address_o      = '0;
71:         we_o           = 1'b0;
72:         en_o           = 1'b0;
73: 
74:         case (state_q)
75:             // we are ready to accept a new request
76:             IDLE: begin
77:                 // we've git a valid write request, we also know that we have asserted the aw_ready
78:                 if (axi_req_i.aw_valid) begin
79:                     axi_resp_o.aw_ready = 1'b1;
80:                     // this costs performance but the interconnect does not obey the AXI standard
81:                     // e.g.: we could wait for aw_valid && w_valid to do the transaction.
82:                     state_d = WRITE;
83:                     // save address
84:                     address_n = axi_req_i.aw
